name: Turner v. United States
question: Does Brady v. Maryland, which established that it was a violation of the defendant’s due process for the prosecution to suppress or withhold evidence that is favorable to the defendant and is material to guilt or punishment, require that the convictions in this case be overturned?
description: A case in which the Court determined that the precedent of Brady v. Maryland does not require that the convictions in this case be overturned because the withheld evidence in question is not material under the standard established in Brady.
